title: Gus staining
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"tbbtr","text":"This experiment aims to establish a Gus staining method for detecting the distribution and activity of β-glucuronidase (GUS) in tissues.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c2wrr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: GUS staining solution: Take an appropriate amount of DMSO, Na2CO3, and water, mix thoroughly until completely dissolved, add GUS substrate, then add potassium ferrocyanide, and finally add Triton X-100.
Buffer: Prepare an appropriate amount of MES, Na2CO3, and water, mix thoroughly until completely dissolved, and adjust the pH to 5.0.
Fix the tissue in 10% formaldehyde and store it at 4℃.
Dehydrate the fixed tissue and immerse it in a clearing agent for transparency.
Embed the transparent tissue and make sections.
Place the sections in the GUS staining solution and incubate at 37℃ for 1-2 hours.
Wash with buffer to remove unbound dyes and substrates.
Place the sections in the substrate solution and incubate at 37℃ for 1-2 hours.
Wash with buffer to remove unbound substrate.
Observe the staining results under a microscope and record the distribution and activity of GUS.
Take photos or draw images as needed to record the experimental results.

This experiment established a reliable GUS staining method that can be used to detect the distribution and activity of β-glucuronidase in tissues. It is important to follow proper experimental procedures to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the staining results.
